# docsfy

> AI-powered documentation generator that creates polished static HTML sites from GitHub repositories

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](introduction.md): Overview of docsfy, what it does, how it uses AI CLI tools to generate Mintlify-quality documentation from GitHub repositories, and the core value proposition
- [Quickstart](quickstart.md): Step-by-step guide to get docsfy running locally with Docker Compose, generate documentation for your first repository, and view the results
- [Installation](installation.md): Detailed installation options including Docker-based deployment, local development setup with uv and Python 3.12+, and system prerequisites like git, Node.js, and AI CLI tools

## Configuration

- [Environment Variables](environment-variables.md): Complete reference of all environment variables including AI_PROVIDER, AI_MODEL, AI_CLI_TIMEOUT, authentication keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, CURSOR_API_KEY), Vertex AI settings, and LOG_LEVEL
- [AI Providers](ai-providers.md): How to configure and switch between supported AI providers: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Cursor Agent, including binary requirements, authentication methods, and model selection
- [Storage & Data](storage-and-data.md): How docsfy stores data using SQLite for metadata and the filesystem for generated documentation, including the directory structure under /data/projects/ and database schema

## Usage

- [Generating Documentation](generating-docs.md): How to submit a repository for documentation generation via the POST /api/generate endpoint, monitor generation status, and understand the four-stage pipeline (clone, plan, content, render)
- [Serving Documentation](serving-docs.md): How to access generated documentation through the built-in static file server at /docs/{project}/, including navigation, dark/light theme toggle, and client-side search
- [Downloading & Self-Hosting](downloading-and-self-hosting.md): How to download generated documentation as a .tar.gz archive via the API and host it on your own infrastructure, CDN, or static hosting service
- [Incremental Updates](incremental-updates.md): How docsfy detects repository changes via commit SHA tracking, re-evaluates documentation plans, and regenerates only affected pages for efficient updates

## Architecture

- [Generation Pipeline](generation-pipeline.md): Deep dive into the four-stage generation pipeline: repository cloning, AI-driven documentation planning (plan.json), concurrent page content generation with semaphore-limited concurrency, and HTML rendering with Jinja2
- [HTML Renderer & Theming](html-renderer.md): How the HTML renderer converts markdown and plan.json into static sites with sidebar navigation, syntax highlighting via highlight.js, callout boxes, card layouts, responsive design, and search indexing
- [AI CLI Integration](ai-cli-integration.md): Technical details of the provider pattern for AI CLI tools, subprocess invocation, async execution via asyncio.to_thread, multi-strategy JSON response parsing, and availability checking

## API Reference

- [POST /api/generate](api-generate.md): Start documentation generation for a GitHub repository URL, including request format, response structure, and supported repository types (public/private, SSH/HTTPS)
- [GET /api/status & Project Details](api-status-and-projects.md): List all projects and their generation status, get detailed project information including last generated timestamp, commit SHA, and page list, and delete projects
- [GET /api/projects/{name}/download](api-download.md): Download a project's generated documentation site as a .tar.gz archive for self-hosting
- [GET /health](api-health.md): Health check endpoint for monitoring and container orchestration

## Deployment

- [Docker Deployment](docker-deployment.md): Production deployment using the multi-stage Dockerfile with python:3.12-slim, non-root user configuration for OpenShift compatibility, volume mounts for data persistence and cloud credentials, and health check configuration
- [Docker Compose Setup](docker-compose.md): Running docsfy with docker-compose.yaml including port mapping, environment file configuration, volume mounts for data and gcloud credentials, and health check settings

## Development

- [Development Setup](development-setup.md): Setting up a local development environment with uv, Python 3.12+, pre-commit hooks (ruff, mypy, flake8, gitleaks, detect-secrets), and tox for testing
- [Contributing](contributing.md): Guidelines for contributing to docsfy including code style enforcement via pre-commit, running tests, build system (hatchling), and project conventions
